chapters.title: — Cold Open: What Changed This Week
— Headline: Why Single-Arm Is the Right Design Here
— Headline: Effect Sizes and What They Mean Clinically
— Headline: Practice Impact and the Affectionate Disagreement
— Close: What to Watch, What's Unsettled

follow_up_links.reason: First pooled evidence quantifying the cerebrovascular and pulmonary hemodynamic response to maternal hyperoxygenation in fetal CHD
follow_up_links.title: Effects of Maternal Hyperoxygenation on Fetal Hemodynamics Assessed by Ultrasound and Fetal Echocardiography in Congenital Heart Disease: A Systematic Review and Single Arm Meta-Analysis

follow_up_links.reason: Prospective reassurance that vasopressor-supported general anesthesia does not compromise uteroplacental Doppler parameters during open fetal surgery
follow_up_links.title: Impact of general anesthesia with vasopressor support on uteroplacental perfusion during fetal spina bifida repair

follow_up_links.reason: Documents that nearly a third of early conotruncal diagnoses evolve morphologically, with early biometry potentially predicting progression
follow_up_links.title: Prenatal Diagnosis of Conotruncal Anomalies Before 16 Weeks: Longitudinal Evolution, Genetic Findings and Early Prognostic Markers

follow_up_links.reason: Largest validation of celocentesis, demonstrating high technical success and diagnostic accuracy for very early monogenic disease testing
follow_up_links.title: Celocentesis for Prenatal Diagnosis of Rare Monogenic Diseases: Development, Validation, and Clinical Outcome on 876 Cases

follow_up_links.reason: Proposes a classification system for an underrecognized neurosonographic finding with significant genetic and infectious associations
follow_up_links.title: Morphological patterns of fetal lateral ventricular border irregularities: descriptive study

key_claims.claim: Maternal hyperoxygenation reduces fetal MCA pulsatility index Z-score and increases pulmonary VTI in CHD fetuses, indicating cerebrovascular and pulmonary hemodynamic responsiveness
General anesthesia with vasopressor support during open fetal myelomeningocele repair preserves uteroplacental Doppler parameters
Nearly 30 percent of conotruncal anomalies diagnosed before 16 weeks undergo morphological progression, and early great-artery biometry may predict which cases will progress
Celocentesis at 7 to 9 weeks achieves 98 percent technical success with 100 percent sensitivity and specificity for monogenic disease diagnosis
Four distinct morphological patterns of fetal lateral ventricular border irregularities can be identified, with half of cases terminated and genetic or infectious etiologies characterized
